maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. Missouri statute does not specify a maximum term for a public school district's contract with a private student-transportation contractor. The board has general authority to make 'all needful rules and regulations for the transportation of pupils' (RSMo 167.251), but no provision in Chapter 167 (Pupils and Special Services), Chapter 162 (School Districts), Chapter 177 (Educational Facilities and Property), or any school-applicable provision in Chapter 34 (State Purchasing) caps the term of a pupil-transportation services contract. The DESE regulation governing contracted bus transportation, 5 CSR 30-261.010(1)(D), requires written contracts on file but is silent on term length. Notably, RSMo 177.171 (which applies only to the St. Louis metropolitan district and only to 'supplies') affirmatively permits the metropolitan board to 'contract for textbooks or school apparatus for such term of years as it deems proper' — confirming that, where the legislature wanted to address term length, it spoke explicitly and uncapped, while remaining silent for pupil-transportation services statewide.
maxContractTerm.citation: RSMo Chapters 162, 167, 177 (no provision capping a school-district pupil-transportation contract); RSMo 167.251 (general board authority); 5 CSR 30-261.010(1)(D) (written contracts required, no term specified); Missouri State Auditor Report 2006-43, p. 5 (state law 'is silent regarding all other purchases of goods and services at the school district level' beyond construction, insurance, architecture, and banking).

rebidRequired.value: Not required by state law. Missouri has no statute requiring a public school district to competitively rebid a pupil-transportation services contract at term-end. The only competitive-procurement statute that specifically reaches school districts is RSMo 177.086, which requires competitive sealed bids only for school 'construction' expenditures exceeding $50,000 (raised from $15,000 effective 28 Aug 2019). RSMo 177.171 applies to the St. Louis metropolitan district and reaches 'supplies' (with a $5,000 small-purchase threshold), not transportation services. RSMo 34.040 (state-purchasing competitive-bid rule) governs state-agency purchases ($10,000+ threshold), not school-district purchases. The Missouri State Auditor's official audit report 2006-43 expressly concludes that 'State law requires construction expenditures exceeding $15,000 to be competitively bid and, with the exception of insurance, architectural and banking services, is silent regarding all other purchases of goods and services at the school district level. Each school district is allowed to establish and implement its own procurement policies and procedures.' DESE guidance ('What must school bus contracts include?') sets contract content requirements but imposes no bidding obligation. Districts therefore proceed under their own locally adopted purchasing policies; the MSBA model purchasing policy DJF-AP1 (2017) enumerates every state-mandated competitive-bidding requirement (construction [177.086], architectural/engineering/land surveying [8.285-.291], construction management [8.675-.687, 67.5050, .5060], auditing [5 CSR 30-4.030], health/life insurance every 3 years [67.150], other insurance every 6 years [376.696], depositories every 5 years [165.201-.291], food service [7 CFR 210.16 and 5 CSR 30-680.010], E-Rate [47 CFR 54.503], coal [34.080]) and does not list pupil-transportation services.
rebidRequired.citation: RSMo 177.086 (construction-only competitive-bid mandate, $50,000 threshold); RSMo 177.171 (metropolitan-district supplies; St. Louis only); RSMo 34.040 (state purchasing; does not apply to school districts); Missouri State Auditor, School District Purchasing Practices, Report 2006-43 (July 2006), pp. 3, 5; DESE, 'What must school bus contracts include?' (citing 5 CSR 30-261.010); MSBA Model Policy DJF-AP1 (Nov. 2017), Legal Compliance section.

renewalMechanics.value: N/A as a matter of statewide statute. Missouri law neither requires nor restricts renewal of school-district pupil-transportation contracts. RSMo 167.251 authorizes the board to make 'all needful rules and regulations' for pupil transportation, and RSMo 432.070 requires that any school-district contract be in writing, dated, and signed by authorized parties, but neither provision addresses renewal mechanics (one-year extensions, CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, automatic rollover, mutual written agreement, agency approval, or board approval). Renewal mechanisms are therefore entirely a matter of local board policy and the four corners of the negotiated contract. State Board of Education approval is not required; DESE's role under 5 CSR 30-261.010 and 5 CSR 30-261.040 concerns operational standards and state-aid cost eligibility, not renewal.
renewalMechanics.citation: RSMo 167.251; RSMo 432.070 (school-district contracts must be in writing); 5 CSR 30-261.010 (contracted-bus operating requirements; silent on renewal); 5 CSR 30-261.040 (state transportation aid; silent on renewal). No Missouri statute or regulation prescribes renewal mechanics for pupil-transportation contracts.

statutoryCitation.value: Primary authorities: RSMo 167.231 (duty to transport pupils more than 3.5 miles from school; permissive transportation for others); RSMo 167.251 (board to prescribe rules and regulations for transportation; bond required of persons employed for transportation; transportation expenses paid from incidental fund); RSMo 432.070 (school-district contracts must be in writing, dated, signed); RSMo 177.086 (school district construction expenditures over $50,000 must be competitively bid - NOT applicable to transportation services); 5 CSR 30-261.010 (State Board regulation governing operation of school buses, including those 'privately-owned and operated under contract with any school district,' requiring written contracts on file). No Missouri statute or administrative regulation establishes a maximum contract term, mandatory competitive-bid procedure, rebid-at-term-end requirement, or renewal mechanics for school-district pupil-transportation contracts.

districtVariance.value: Yes. Because Missouri statute is silent on contract term, bidding, and renewal for pupil-transportation contracts, each of Missouri's 524 public school districts establishes its own purchasing policy and contract structure. The Missouri State Auditor's 2006 audit (Report 2006-43) expressly documented this variance: of 15 sampled districts, 'inconsistent district procurement procedures existed,' districts used 'a combination of verbal bids, request-for-proposal sealed bids, advertised bids and select vendor requests,' bid-threshold dollar values varied (where set at all) from $100 to $5,000, and 'each school district is allowed to establish and implement its own procurement policies and procedures.' Observable contracted-out transportation arrangements range from 1-year initial terms with annual renewals (Smithville 2025 RFP - cab/taxi) to 3-year terms (Moberly 2025 RFP for 2026-2029; Meramec Valley R-III 2026 RFP) to 3-year initial terms with 2-year renewal options (Frontier Schools 2021 RFP) to 5-year initial terms (Excelsior Springs 2022-2027) and multi-year evergreen renewals (St. Louis Public Schools / Missouri Central School Bus annual renewals) - all set by individual board contract, not state law. MSBA offers model purchasing policies (DJF / DJF-AP1) that districts may adopt, but those policies only formalize locally chosen procedures; they do not impose a statewide rule.

specialEducation.value: No separate state contracting rule found. Missouri statute and regulation impose no different term limit, rebid requirement, procurement rule, or renewal mechanic for special-education / IEP-mandated pupil transportation contracts compared to regular-route contracts. RSMo 162.710 mandates the duty to transport: 'The district responsible for furnishing special educational services shall provide necessary transportation for all handicapped children residing within the district, including transportation to and from contracted day classes,' but addresses the duty to provide service - not how the district contracts for that service. RSMo 162.670-162.995 generally govern special-education services and DESE's special-education compliance framework requires districts to provide the transportation specified in a student's IEP. State transportation aid for handicapped students is governed by RSMo 163.031 and 5 CSR 30-261.040 (75% of cost), but those are funding rules, not contracting rules. DESE FAQ guidance confirms that special transportation needs are documented in the IEP and that resulting costs may be eligible for state aid; the guidance imposes no separate contracting/bidding regime. Federal IDEA obligations bear on the duty to transport but do not change state contract or procurement rules.
specialEducation.citation: RSMo 162.710 (district duty to provide special-education transportation, including to contracted day classes); RSMo 162.670-162.995 (special-education services framework); RSMo 163.031 and RSMo 163.161 (state aid for transporting students with disabilities); 5 CSR 30-261.040(1)(G) and (2)(A) (allowable costs for state transportation aid, including special-education); DESE, 'What transportation requirements are in place for students with disabilities?'

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: No statutory maximum found.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A Missouri statute, regulation, or DESE guidance somewhere caps the term length of a pupil-transportation services contract (e.g., a 'not to exceed' limit, an annual-appropriation limit, a multi-year-contract limitation, or a textbook/apparatus-like term-of-years provision applied to transportation).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened RSMo 167.231, 167.251, 432.070, 177.086, 177.171, 34.040 via revisor.mo.gov. Reviewed 5 CSR 30-261.010 and .040 (full PDF from Missouri Secretary of State). Searched Missouri Revisor for 'pupil transportation', 'school bus contract', 'term', 'years', 'not exceed', 'renewal'. Searched 2025 and 2026 Missouri General Assembly session bills via legiscan.com and senate.mo.gov. Re-verified Auditor Report 2006-43 (verbatim PDF read) and MSBA DJF-AP1 (verbatim PDF read). RSMo 177.171 expressly allows the metropolitan St. Louis board to 'contract for textbooks or school apparatus for such term of years as it deems proper' - confirming the legislature's silence elsewhere is intentional, not oversight.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. No authority capping the term of a pupil-transportation services contract was located in statute, regulation, AG opinion, or official agency guidance. The only term-of-years language in the entire school-district procurement code is RSMo 177.171's uncapped grant of discretion to the St. Louis metropolitan board for textbooks/apparatus.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - statutory and regulatory silence is documented across all official sources; no counter-evidence at any tier.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End: Not required.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A Missouri school-, procurement-, public-contract-, or district-purchasing statute mandates competitive bidding for school-district services (which would, by definition, capture pupil transportation), or the metropolitan-district statute (RSMo 177.171) somehow reaches transportation, or RSMo 34.040 reaches school districts as 'political subdivisions.'
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened RSMo 177.086 (verbatim - 'construction of facilities' only, $50,000); RSMo 177.171 (verbatim - 'supplies required in the schools', applies only to metropolitan St. Louis district; explicitly NOT services); RSMo 34.040 (verbatim - applies to 'state purchasing,' Commissioner of Administration, state agencies, not school districts). Verified Auditor Report 2006-43 conclusion verbatim: 'State law requires construction expenditures exceeding $15,000 to be competitively bid and, with the exception of insurance, architectural and banking services, is silent regarding all other purchases of goods and services at the school district level.' Verified MSBA DJF-AP1 'Legal Compliance' section verbatim - enumerates 11 categories of state-mandated bidding (construction, architectural/engineering, construction management, auditing, health/life insurance, other insurance, depositories, food service, E-Rate, transactions with board members, $5,000 service-contract E-Verify) - pupil-transportation services is NOT listed. Searched for RSMo 70.220 cooperative-purchasing (permissive only). Searched for sole-source/emergency exceptions (none specific to transportation).
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. No state-law mandate to rebid pupil-transportation services contracts at term-end. Stage 1's finding holds.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - corroborated by primary statutes, the State Auditor's own enumeration of what IS bid-mandated, and the MSBA model policy's parallel enumeration.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Missouri law restricts or conditions renewal (e.g., CPI cap, fuel-escalator limit, agency approval, board-approval form, max number of renewals, annual-appropriations clause, public-hearing requirement) for pupil-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened RSMo 167.251 (board has 'all needful rules and regulations' authority - no renewal terms). Re-opened RSMo 432.070 (writing requirement only). Reviewed 5 CSR 30-261.010 verbatim (silent on renewal). Reviewed 5 CSR 30-261.040 verbatim (state-aid cost eligibility only). Reviewed DESE 'What must school bus contracts include?' (silent on renewal). Searched for 'renewal,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'rollover,' 'evergreen,' 'CPI,' 'escalator' in Missouri school-district procurement law. Reviewed Auditor Report 2006-43 (no renewal regime referenced).
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. No Missouri statute, regulation, or official agency guidance prescribes renewal mechanics for school-district pupil-transportation contracts. Renewal terms are entirely a matter of local board policy and contract drafting.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - statutory and regulatory silence documented; no counter-evidence.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation: No separate state contracting rule.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Missouri law imposes different procurement, term, bid, or renewal rules for special-education / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / homeless-student / disability transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-verified RSMo 162.710 verbatim (duty-to-transport rule only - 'The district responsible for furnishing special educational services shall provide necessary transportation for all handicapped children residing within the district, including transportation to and from contracted day classes' - no procurement/bidding/renewal provision). Reviewed RSMo 162.670-162.995 (special-education framework - no separate contracting rule). Reviewed 5 CSR 30-261.040(1)(G) and (2)(A) verbatim (cost-eligibility rule, not contracting rule). Reviewed DESE 'What transportation requirements are in place for students with disabilities?' verbatim (IEP-documentation rule only). Searched for McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student transportation contracting provisions in Missouri statute (none separate from general framework).
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. Special-education transportation in Missouri follows the same (silent) state procurement framework as regular-route transportation; the difference is in the duty to provide and in the cost-eligibility/funding regime, not in contracting rules.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - the duty/funding distinction is explicit; no separate procurement rule exists at any source tier.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Despite statutory silence, statewide uniform district practice has developed via DESE guidance, MSBA model policy, or a school-boards-association compact that effectively standardizes term length, bidding, or renewal across districts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-verified Missouri State Auditor Report 2006-43 verbatim: 'inconsistent district procurement procedures existed,' districts used 'a combination of verbal bids, request-for-proposal sealed bids, advertised bids and select vendor requests,' bid thresholds 'varied from $100 to $5,000.' Re-verified MSBA Model Policy DJF-AP1 verbatim: it sets a $15,000 sealed-bid threshold for the adopting district, but explicitly does not list pupil-transportation services among state-mandated bid categories - meaning each adopting district decides how the policy reaches transportation. Observed district contract structures in 2021-2026 RFPs: Smithville (1-year + annual renewals), Moberly (3-year), Meramec Valley R-III (3-year), Frontier Schools (3+2 renewals), Excelsior Springs (5-year), St. Louis Public Schools (annual renewals with rate escalator). Each is a different structure under the same state silence - variance is empirically observable and documented by the State Auditor.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. District variance is real, documented by the State Auditor as a finding, and corroborated by the empirical range of district RFP structures.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - State Auditor explicitly documents inter-district variance; observable RFP evidence corroborates.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: One or more cited sections was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the recorded effective date; or a 2025-2026 session law modified the school-district transportation procurement framework.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened each section live at revisor.mo.gov on 24 June 2026 and confirmed current text. Effective dates verified: 167.231 (28 Aug 2006), 167.251 (28 Aug 1963), 432.070 (28 Aug 2007), 177.086 (28 Aug 2019), 177.171 (28 Aug 1981), 34.040 (28 Aug 2019). Searched Missouri Senate 2026 Bill List and legiscan.com 2025-2026 session for any enacted bill amending RSMo 167.*, 177.086, 177.171, 432.070, or 34.040 with respect to pupil-transportation contracting - none located. 2026 Missouri General Assembly adjourned sine die 15 May 2026.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. All cited sections are current as of access date; no post-currency amendment changes the analysis.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: High - current-law check documented across all primary citations.

sourcesReVerified: RSMo 162.710 (revisor.mo.gov - verbatim re-read; confirms duty-to-transport, not contracting rule)
5 CSR 30-261.010 (sos.mo.gov - verbatim re-read of full title PDF; subsection (1)(D) on-file contracts requirement confirmed)
5 CSR 30-261.040 (sos.mo.gov - verbatim re-read of full title PDF; cost-eligibility rule for contracted and special-education transportation confirmed)
Missouri State Auditor Report 2006-43 (auditor.mo.gov - verbatim re-read of all relevant pages; quotations confirmed exact)
DESE 'What must school bus contracts include?' (dese.mo.gov - re-read; quotation confirmed)
DESE 'What transportation requirements are in place for students with disabilities?' (dese.mo.gov - re-read; confirms IEP-documentation regime, no separate contracting rule)
MSBA Model Policy DJF-AP1 (verbatim PDF re-read of all 14 pages; Legal Compliance enumeration confirmed; pupil-transportation services NOT among 11 state-mandated bid categories)
sourcesUnreachable: DESE School Transportation Administrator's Handbook (Nov. 2008) PDF via ERIC - file was returned but PDF text extraction failed (binary encoding); citation retained as a Tier-4 compilation source but its contents could not be independently re-verified word-for-word in this validation pass. The substantive findings it would support (RSMo 432.070 as the only contract-formation rule; no separate transportation-contracting statute) are independently verified through the primary-source re-reads above, so the unverifiable status of this single secondary compilation does not change any finding.

sourceLedger.citation: RSMo 167.231 - Transportation of pupils by districts, except metropolitan
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 2006; verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Establishes duty to transport pupils 3.5+ miles from school; framework statute; silent on contract term, rebid, renewal
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Within all school districts except metropolitan districts the board of education shall provide transportation to and from school for all pupils living more than three and one-half miles from school and may provide transportation for all pupils.'

sourceLedger.citation: RSMo 167.251 - Board to prescribe rules and regulations - driver to give bond
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 1963; verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: General board authority over pupil transportation; bond required of transportation employees; silent on contract term, rebid, renewal
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'When transportation is provided by a district pursuant to law, the school board shall make all needful rules and regulations for the transportation of pupils and shall require from every person employed for that purpose, a reasonable bond conditioned upon the faithful discharge of his duties as prescribed by the board... Expenses of transportation shall be paid out of the incidental fund of the district.'

sourceLedger.citation: RSMo 432.070 - Contracts, execution of by counties, towns - form of contract
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 2007; verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: All school-district contracts (including transportation) must be in writing, dated, and signed by authorized parties
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'No county, city, town, village, school township, school district or other municipal corporation shall make any contract, unless the same shall be within the scope of its powers or be expressly authorized by law... and such contract, including the consideration, shall be in writing and dated when made, and shall be subscribed by the parties thereto, or their agents authorized by law and duly appointed and authorized in writing.'

sourceLedger.citation: RSMo 177.086 - Construction of facilities, sealed bids and public advertisement required, when
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 2019 (threshold raised to $50,000); verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Only school-district-specific competitive-bid mandate; limited to CONSTRUCTION over $50,000; does NOT reach pupil-transportation services contracts (negative authority)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Any school district authorizing the construction of facilities which may exceed an expenditure of fifty thousand dollars shall publicly advertise... for bids on said construction... all contracts shall be let to the lowest responsible bidder complying with the terms of the letting...'

sourceLedger.citation: RSMo 177.171 - Metropolitan school district purchasing (St. Louis)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 1981; verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Applies to metropolitan (St. Louis) district 'supplies' over $5,000, not transportation services; permits 'contract for textbooks or school apparatus for such term of years as it deems proper' (no cap)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The board of education shall cause advertisements to be made under regulations that it provides for proposals for furnishing the supplies required in the schools and by the board. Every contract shall be awarded to the lowest responsible bidder complying with the terms of the letting; but the board shall have and reserve the right to reject any and all bids. The board may authorize the purchase of supplies not exceeding five thousand dollars in amount without letting of contract... The board may contract for textbooks or school apparatus for such term of years as it deems proper.'

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 28 Aug 2019; verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: State-agency purchasing rule ($10,000+ competitively bid); negative authority - does NOT apply to school districts (governs Commissioner of Administration, state purchasing law)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'All purchases in excess of ten thousand dollars shall be based on competitive bids, except as otherwise provided in this chapter.' (State-agency scope only.)

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Verified current at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Duty to provide special-education transportation; NOT a contracting/bidding/renewal rule (supports specialEducation field)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The district responsible for furnishing special educational services shall provide necessary transportation for all handicapped children residing within the district, including transportation to and from contracted day classes, notwithstanding the provisions of sections 162.621 and 167.231.'

sourceLedger.citation: 5 CSR 30-261.010 - Requirements for the Operation of School Buses
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current Missouri Code of State Regulations as published by Secretary of State Ashcroft (7/31/23); last amended Nov. 29, 2018, effective June 30, 2019; verified 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Requires written contracts for privately-owned-and-operated school buses under contract with any district; on-file requirement; silent on term, rebid, renewal
sourceLedger.relevantText: PURPOSE: 'The State Board of Education has the authority to adopt and enforce regulations for the operation of all school buses used for the transportation of schoolchildren when owned and operated by any school district or privately-owned and operated under contract with any school district in this state.' Subsection (1)(D): 'There shall be on file in the appropriate school district office copies of current contracts, bonds, driver's physical examination certificates, driver's license with applicable endorsements, and maps of school bus routes.'

sourceLedger.citation: 5 CSR 30-261.040 - Allowable Costs for State Transportation Aid
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current Missouri CSR (Secretary of State); last amended Nov. 28, 2006, effective June 30, 2007; verified 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: State-aid cost-eligibility regime including for contracted-out and special-ed transportation; not a contracting rule
sourceLedger.relevantText: PURPOSE: 'Section 163.161, RSMo authorizes state transportation aid to school districts based upon allowable costs. This rule defines allowable costs and specifies how they will be used in calculating state transportation aid.' (2)(A) Contracted school transportation: 'Contracted transportation costs, including transportation of students under the provisions of section 304.060, RSMo administrative support services in subsection (1)(I) of this rule and costs paid to other school districts, are allowable.'

sourceLedger.citation: DESE - 'What must school bus contracts include?'
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current DESE guidance page; verified 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms only required content (driver/vehicle compliance, reimbursement, insurance, ridership/mileage tracking) and on-file requirement; imposes no term-length, bidding, or renewal rule
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Written contracts are required for all contracted bus transportation services. The contracts should include requirements that the driver(s) and vehicle(s) meet all applicable state statutes and State Board of Education regulations. This department also suggests that the contract include the reimbursement amount, the insurance required by the school district, and that the contract requires the tracking of ridership and mileage. The contract must be on file in the school district's office (5 CSR 30-261.010).'

sourceLedger.citation: DESE - 'What transportation requirements are in place for students with disabilities?'
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current DESE guidance page; verified 24 Jun 2026
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation - confirms IEP-documentation regime, no separate contracting/bidding/renewal rule
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Students with disabilities who have special transportation needs must have those needs detailed in the student's IEP (Individualized Educational Plan) under Related Services. These needs may include the requirement for an aide on the bus with the student, curb-to-curb service, transportation for a student with disabilities living less than one mile from school, and transportation for special education summer school. If written into the student's IEP these expenses are considered eligible for state transportation aid.'

sourceLedger.citation: Missouri State Auditor, School District Purchasing Practices, Report No. 2006-43 (July 2006), Claire McCaskill, State Auditor
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Issued July 2006; statutory landscape described still substantially controls (no new statute or DESE regulation on school-district transportation-contract bidding has been adopted since); 2019 amendment to RSMo 177.086 raised construction threshold from $15,000 to $50,000 but did not extend to transportation services
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Authoritative state-auditor conclusion that Missouri law is silent on school-district competitive bidding for goods and services other than construction, insurance, architecture, and banking; documents inter-district variance
sourceLedger.relevantText: p. 3 (Current procurement statutes): 'The most significant procurement statute relevant to school districts is section 177.086, RSMo, which requires school district officials to competitively select, through public advertisement for bids, construction expenditures of $15,000 or higher.' p. 5: 'State law requires construction expenditures exceeding $15,000 to be competitively bid and, with the exception of insurance, architectural and banking services, is silent regarding all other purchases of goods and services at the school district level. Each school district is allowed to establish and implement its own procurement policies and procedures.' p. 5: 'For other goods, inconsistent district procurement procedures existed. For example districts used a combination of verbal bids, request-for-proposal sealed bids, advertised bids and select vendor requests. Only two districts visited had formally established a dollar value for purchases requiring a bid, but those thresholds varied from $100 to $5,000 and had been inconsistently applied.'

sourceLedger.citation: Missouri School Boards' Association Model Policy DJF-AP1 (Purchasing), Implemented 08/11/2003, Revised 11/16/2017
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: MSBA 2017; cites RSMo provisions as of 2017 (construction threshold shown as $15,000 reflects pre-2019 figure but underlying statute citation remains correct)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: MSBA model purchasing policy 'Legal Compliance' section enumerates EVERY state-mandated competitive-bidding requirement for school districts: (1) construction $15,000+ (RSMo 107.170, 177.086, 285.530, 290.210-.340, 292.675); (2) architectural/engineering/land surveying (RSMo 8.285-.291); (3) construction management (RSMo 8.675-.687, 67.5050, .5060); (4) auditing (5 CSR 30-4.030); (5) health/life insurance every 3 years (RSMo 67.150); (6) other insurance every 6 years (RSMo 376.696); (7) depositories every 5 years (RSMo 165.201-.291); (8) food service management (7 CFR 210.16, 5 CSR 30-680.010); (9) E-Rate (47 CFR 54.503); (10) board-member transactions (RSMo 105.454, .458, 171.181); (11) $5,000+ service contracts must include E-Verify affidavit (RSMo 285.530). Pupil-transportation services are NOT listed - corroborating that no state mandate exists.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Legal Compliance section (p. 8-9): '1. All construction projects that may exceed an expenditure of $15,000 shall be advertised in a newspaper of general circulation and competitively bid, in accordance with law... See 107.170, 177.086, 285.530, 290.210-.340, 292.675, RSMo. 2. All purchasing of architectural, engineering or land surveying services must be advertised, bid and selected in accordance with Board policy and law. See 8.285-.291, RSMo... 5. Health and life insurance contracts will be competitively bid at least every three years. See 67.150, RSMo. 6. General liability and other forms of insurance contracts will be competitively bid at least every six years. See 376.696, RSMo. 7. Depositories of district funds will be competitively bid at least every five years. See 165.201 - .291, RSMo. 8. The selection of food service management companies will be made in accordance with bidding requirements in state and federal law. See 7 C.F.R. 210.16; 5 C.S.R. 30-680.010.' [Pupil-transportation services - not enumerated anywhere in the Legal Compliance section.]

sourceLedger.citation: DESE School Transportation Administrator's Handbook (Revised November 2008; available via ERIC ED518912)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Compiled November 2008; reproduces RSMo 167.231, 167.241, 167.251, 432.070, and the 5 CSR 30-261 series. Note: this validation pass could not extract verbatim text from the PDF due to binary encoding; the substantive findings the handbook would support are independently verified through primary-source re-reads.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Compiles Missouri statutes and regulations relevant to pupil transportation; the absence of any transportation-bidding/term/renewal statute is independently confirmed through direct primary-source verification (re-verified through revisor.mo.gov and sos.mo.gov in this pass).
sourceLedger.relevantText: Per Stage 1 description (could not be re-verified verbatim in this pass; flagged as 'sourceUnreachable' - primary sources independently verified): Handbook reproduces RSMo 432.070 ('Contracts Required to be in Writing') as the only contract-formation rule applicable; does not cite or reference any competitive-bidding or term-limit statute for pupil-transportation services.

queriesThatMattered: Direct re-reads at revisor.mo.gov of RSMo 167.231, 167.251, 432.070, 177.086, 177.171, 34.040, and 162.710 - all verbatim verified on 24 Jun 2026

Verbatim PDF re-read of 5 CSR 30-261 series via Missouri Secretary of State (sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5c30-261.pdf) - confirms (1)(D) on-file contracts requirement and (2)(A) contracted-transportation cost eligibility; silent on term, rebid, renewal

Verbatim PDF re-read of Missouri State Auditor Report 2006-43 (auditor.mo.gov/press/2006-43.pdf) - confirms 'silent regarding all other purchases of goods and services at the school district level' conclusion and inter-district variance findings
Verbatim PDF re-read of MSBA Model Policy DJF-AP1 (all 14 pages) - confirms 'Legal Compliance' enumeration of every state-mandated bid category; pupil-transportation services not listed
DESE 'What must school bus contracts include?' page re-fetched - confirms only content/on-file rule, no bid/term/renewal mandate
DESE 'What transportation requirements are in place for students with disabilities?' page re-fetched - confirms IEP-documentation regime, no separate contracting rule
RSMo 162.710 verified verbatim - duty-to-transport statute, not a contracting rule (strengthens specialEducation finding)
2026 Missouri General Assembly session bill list (senate.mo.gov, legiscan.com) - confirmed sine die adjournment 15 May 2026 with no enacted bill amending school-district transportation contracting
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: RSMo accessed via official Missouri Revisor of Statutes at revisor.mo.gov on 24 Jun 2026. Effective dates verified: 167.231 (28 Aug 2006); 167.251 (28 Aug 1963); 432.070 (28 Aug 2007); 177.086 (28 Aug 2019, threshold raised to $50,000); 177.171 (28 Aug 1981); 34.040 (28 Aug 2019); 162.710 (current).
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: 5 CSR 30-261.010 last amended Nov. 29, 2018, effective June 30, 2019; 5 CSR 30-261.040 last amended Nov. 28, 2006, effective June 30, 2007; both confirmed current via Missouri Secretary of State CSR publication signed by John R. Ashcroft (7/31/23 cover date); verified 24 Jun 2026.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Checked 2025 and 2026 Missouri General Assembly sessions for any bill amending or adding a statute on school-district transportation contracting. 2026 session adjourned sine die 15 May 2026; no bill substantively amending RSMo 167.*, 177.086, 177.171, 432.070, 34.040, or 162.710 with respect to contract term, bidding, or renewal for pupil-transportation services was identified via senate.mo.gov bill list or legiscan.com Missouri 2026 search.
negativeSearchPaths: Searched Missouri Revisor of Statutes (revisor.mo.gov) for: 'school bus contract term', 'pupil transportation contract', 'student transportation services contract', 'school district transportation bid', 'transportation contract renewal' - no responsive statute beyond the framework provisions (167.231, 167.251, 432.070) and the construction-only bid mandate (177.086).
Searched RSMo Chapter 167 (Pupils and Special Services) section-by-section - confirmed no transportation-contracting-procedure section exists.
Searched RSMo Chapter 162 (School Districts) including 162.710 - duty-to-transport for special education, NOT a contracting rule.

Searched RSMo Chapter 177 (Educational Facilities and Property) - 177.086 reaches construction only ($50,000); 177.171 reaches metropolitan-district (St. Louis) supplies only ($5,000); neither reaches transportation services.
Searched RSMo Chapter 34 (State Purchasing) - applies to state agencies under the Commissioner of Administration, not school districts.
Searched Missouri Code of State Regulations Title 5, Division 30, Chapter 261 (entire pupil-transportation chapter) - operational/safety/aid rules only; no contract-term, bid, or renewal rule.
Searched Missouri Attorney General Opinions (ago.mo.gov via web search) - no opinion identified addressing school-district transportation contract term length, rebid, or renewal mechanics.

Searched DESE FAQ pages (all pages of /faq-categorization/school-transportation) and DESE Student Transportation pages - guidance specifies only contract content and on-file requirements; no rule on term, bid, or renewal.
Searched Missouri 2025 and 2026 session laws via legiscan.com and senate.mo.gov bill list - no enacted bill addressing the topic.
Searched MSBA model purchasing policy (DJF-AP1) Legal Compliance section - confirmed it enumerates all 11 state-mandated bid categories and pupil-transportation services is not among them.
Searched for cooperative-purchasing authority (RSMo 70.220) - permissive intergovernmental cooperation only; does not impose a transportation-contracting regime.
Searched for sole-source/emergency procurement exceptions (RSMo 34.044, 34.045) - state-agency rules; school districts handle exceptions through local policy (e.g., MSBA DJF-AP1 sections 2 and 6).
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict among authoritative sources identified. Primary statutes (revisor.mo.gov), State Board regulations (Missouri Secretary of State CSR publication), the State Auditor's official 2006 report, DESE FAQ guidance, and the MSBA model purchasing policy all consistently reflect that Missouri imposes no statewide term limit, competitive-bid requirement, or renewal mechanic on school-district pupil-transportation services contracts. The 2017 MSBA policy shows the pre-2019 $15,000 construction threshold; the current statutory threshold is $50,000 per the 2019 amendment to RSMo 177.086, but this does not affect the substantive analysis (construction-only mandate, not transportation).
